title: hypochaeris maculata subsp. pelivanovicii
intro: Hypochaeris maculata subsp. pelivanovicii, also known as spotted cat's ear or spotted hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Hypochaeris maculata subsp. pelivanovicii include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and helping to alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis. It is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
